citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
An adult female Pere David's deer was observed with swelling beneath her right eye. The swelling encompassed all of her
lower eyelid and extended towards the top of her cheek and inner corner of the right eye. The eye was open and her
vision was not impaired. No visible discharge, redness, bruising, nor open wounds were seen. The facility had not
observed the swelling prior to inspection.
Swelling that expands underneath the eye can cause pain and unnecessary discomfort. Inadequate daily observations
inhibit adequate monitoring for changes to an animals health. This results in delay of care and potentially to prolonged
suffering.
Each dealer or exhibitor shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include daily observation
of all animals to assess their health and well-being. A mechanism of direct and frequent communication is required so that
timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed to the attending
veterinarian.
Correct by 5/26/26.

There were no records of acquisition, disposition, or records of animals on hand available at the time of inspection for any
of the regulated animals present at the facility.
A complete record of animals is required to trace and track animals being used in regulated activities to ensure their
humane care and use.
Every dealer and exhibitor shall make, keep and maintain records or forms that fully and correctly disclose the required
information, which includes: the name and address of the person from whom the animals were acquired, the USDA
license number of the person if licensed or the vehicle license number and state and driver’s license number and state if
not licensed, the name and address of the person to whom an animal was given/sold, the date of acquisition or
disposition, the species of animal and the number of animals involved. The records shall include any offspring born of any
animal while in his or her possession or under his or her control.
Correct by 6/5/2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
An adult female Pere David's deer was observed with swelling beneath her right eye. The swelling encompassed all of her 
lower eyelid and extended towards the top of her cheek and inner corner of the right eye. The eye was open and her 
vision was not impaired. No visible discharge, redness, bruising, nor open wounds were seen. The facility had not 
observed the swelling prior to inspection. 
 
Swelling that expands underneath the eye can cause pain and unnecessary discomfort. Inadequate daily observations 
inhibit adequate monitoring for changes to an animals health. This results in delay of care and potentially to prolonged 
suffering.  
 
Each dealer or exhibitor shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include daily observation 
of all animals to assess their health and well-being. A mechanism of direct and frequent communication is required so that 
timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed to the attending 
veterinarian. 
 
Correct by 5/26/26. 
2.75(b)(1) 
   
Records: Dealers and exhibitors. 
There were no records of acquisition, disposition, or records of animals on hand available at the time of inspection for any 
of the regulated animals present at the facility. 
 
A complete record of animals is required to trace and track animals being used in regulated activities to ensure their 
humane care and use.  
 
Every dealer and exhibitor shall make, keep and maintain records or forms that fully and correctly disclose the required 
information, which includes: the name and address of the person from whom the animals were acquired, the USDA 
license number of the person if licensed or the vehicle license number and state and driver’s license number and state if 
not licensed, the name and address of the person to whom an animal was given/sold, the date of acquisition or

disposition, the species of animal and the number of animals involved. The records shall include any offspring born of any 
animal while in his or her possession or under his or her control. 
 
Correct by 6/5/2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
